AC-CORR-01.why: §3.6 correction is an immediate attributed patch to the durable entry store (§3.3 note_deltas/Postgres); oracle asserts current_value==V1 and superseded V0 both in Postgres.

AC-CORR-01-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-01; asserts an incomplete correction is not committed to Postgres as a complete record — same durable-store class.

AC-CORR-02.why: §3.6 superseded-not-erased audit trail; oracle queries superseded records [V0,V1] and current V2 in Postgres.

AC-CORR-02-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-02; asserts in-place overwrite (no supersede row) is detected in Postgres state — same class.

AC-CORR-03-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-03 (which is db:postgres in-file); a correction that fails to commit to Postgres leaves the Scribe prefix showing V0 — durable-store dependent.

AC-CORR-04.why: §3.6 ordinary firmness bump applies silently-immediate; oracle asserts entry.firmness==new value in Postgres (speak path only mocked, not the durable write).

AC-CORR-04-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-04; a firmness-bump correction whose Postgres write fails must emit no false confirm — the write under test is Postgres.

AC-CORR-05.why: §3.6 action-item tweak on the silent-immediate path; oracle asserts action_item corrected value committed to Postgres.

AC-CORR-05-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-05; a tweak that also sets Decision.status=final is reclassified high-stakes — same durable-store class as its positive.

AC-CORR-06.why: §3.6 forming-decision lean stays on silent-immediate path; oracle asserts decision.status=='forming' committed in Postgres.

AC-CORR-07.why: §3.6 open-question edit stays silent-immediate; oracle asserts question.status=='open' committed in Postgres after patch.

AC-CORR-08.why: §3.6 high-stakes gate: patch (Decision.status=final) committed to Postgres BEFORE the spoken confirm; oracle traces db_commit happens-before speak, plus superseded row in Postgres.

AC-CORR-08-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-08; forced Postgres write failure must emit no false confirm — the failing write under test is Postgres.

AC-CORR-09.why: §3.6 high-stakes gate on Reversibility=irreversible: patch committed to Postgres before confirm; oracle traces db_commit happens-before speak, superseded row persisted.

AC-CORR-09-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-09; forced Postgres write failure → no false confirm — durable-store write under test.

AC-CORR-10.why: §3.6 gate fires on closing/finalizing an already-final entry; oracle asserts the patch commit occurs in Postgres alongside the single spoken confirm.

AC-CORR-10-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CORR-10; second close of an already-final entry still commits to Postgres and fires the gate — same class.

AC-CORR-11.why: §3.6 confirm is an audible receipt, not a blocking approval; oracle is a pure pipeline state-machine property (no approval_pending/wait state, next-delta timing) verifiable in-process — touches no external system.

AC-CORR-12.why: §3.6 boundary: notes gate governs notes only; oracle asserts in-process routing booleans (notes_gate_invoked==False, human_click path fires, speak count 0) — pure state-machine, no external system.

AC-CLOSE-01.why: §3.7 the one strong-model close pass producing the permanent record end-to-end; headline is 'exactly one generateStructured call to the Sonnet-class model' driving the whole model→GCS→chat→teardown chain. THE golden-path close flow (multi-system: also db/gcs/recall; primary = the strong-model pass that is the substance of §3.7).

AC-CLOSE-02.why: §3.7 meeting-end signal is the sole trigger, initiated exactly once; pure trigger state-machine (in-file dependency_class []), no external system under test.

AC-CLOSE-02-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-02; asserts silence does not trigger the close pass — same in-process trigger state-machine, no external system.

AC-CLOSE-03.why: §3.7 close pass is a Sonnet-class (PROXY_MODEL_SCRIBE_CLOSE) pass, never Haiku; cassette at call_external asserts request.model — the Anthropic call is under test.

AC-CLOSE-03-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-03; the model-tier guard for the Anthropic close call — same vendor class as its positive.

AC-CLOSE-04.why: §3.7 reduces over the folded ledger, NOT full raw transcript; primary oracle is a cassette assertion on the Anthropic prompt (folded ledger + exactly 5 gap/pending spans, no comprehended raw text). Also reads Postgres, but the property under test is prompt composition at the model seam.

AC-CLOSE-04-NEG.why: Though the AC-CLOSE-04 pair is vendor:anthropic, this NEG specifically tests the gap/pending-span read failing when Postgres is unreachable (§3.7 backfill from note_deltas/transcript_segments); the durable-store fetch is the primary system under test.

AC-CLOSE-05.why: §3.7 chunk-reduce only when the folded ledger exceeds a token threshold — a normal meeting folds in one pass; below-threshold single-call control-flow is pure in-process logic (in-file dependency_class []).

AC-CLOSE-05B.why: §3.7 chunk-reduce path above the token threshold; the branch decision (model_call_count>1, still one merged object) is pure in-process control-flow — no external system directly under test.

AC-CLOSE-06.why: §3.7 close pass runs as generateStructured with outputFormat json_schema (not raw messages.create); cassette at the Anthropic call_external seam is the oracle.

AC-CLOSE-06-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-06; §3.7 typed terminal error_max_turns from the generateStructured call — same Anthropic vendor class.

AC-CLOSE-06-NEG2.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-06 (per rule); §3.7 distinct typed error_max_structured_output_retries from the generateStructured call — same Anthropic vendor class.

AC-CLOSE-07.why: §3.7 single operation_runs row operation_type='meeting-close', NO close_jobs table; db_assertion over operation_runs and information_schema — Postgres is the system under test.

AC-CLOSE-07-NEG.why: §3.7 create-only (if_generation_match=0) write exactly once; a second close attempt is rejected by the GCS precondition — the GCS create-only guard is the primary mechanism preventing the duplicate (also touches Postgres/operation_runs).

AC-CLOSE-08.why: §3.7 write_finalized_notes with if_generation_match=0 (create-only, written exactly once); integration assertion on the GCS request params and created object generation.

AC-CLOSE-08-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-08; GCS unavailable → close halts before chat link/teardown (§3.7 durable URL must exist first) — GCS is the failing system under test.

AC-CLOSE-09.why: §3.7 mandatory order render→GCS write→post chat link→tear down; the GCS write-confirm is the ordering anchor between render and the chat post (also touches recall for the chat post; primary = the GCS write pivot).

AC-CLOSE-10.why: §3.7 post the link in the meeting chat THEN tear down — the bot never leaves without the record posted; retry on the chat-link post is a Recall (Doc 04 chat) call at the call_external seam.

AC-CLOSE-10-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-10; permanent chat-post failure blocks teardown and surfaces error — the failing chat post is the Recall vendor call.

AC-CLOSE-11.why: §3.7/§3.9 total_cost_usd is a claude_agent_sdk result convenience the close pass gets for free; oracle reads it from the generateStructured result — the Anthropic SDK call surface is under test.

AC-CLOSE-11-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-11; a missing total_cost_usd field on the SDK result is tolerated — same Anthropic SDK-result vendor class.

AC-CLOSE-12.why: §3.7 close output is deduped, conflicts resolved, gaps backfilled, human-polished; golden_key_comparison scores the strong-model output — the Anthropic generation quality is under test.

AC-CLOSE-13.why: §3.7 raw transcript + workroom tool-call telemetry persist on the Postgres plane as the audit record; db_assertion that row counts are unchanged after teardown.

AC-CLOSE-13-NEG.why: NEG pair of AC-CLOSE-13; asserts no DELETE/TRUNCATE on transcript_segments/note_deltas during close — Postgres audit rows are the system under test.

AC-CLOSE-14.why: §3.7 close deliverable written exactly once; crash recovery detects the existing GCS object via the if_generation_match precondition — the GCS create-only precondition is the primary detection mechanism (also touches Postgres).

AC-CLOSE-15.why: §3.7 build-time pin: the claude_agent_sdk structured-output surface MUST be confirmed against live docs at build; process/code assertion on build evidence — no runtime external call.

AC-CLOSE-16.why: §3.7 V1-deferred items (show-your-work trace, staged-drafts bundle, decisions→index) are absent in V0; code_assertion (grep) — pure static check, no external system.
